value.body:
## Summary
Add first-class support for `cycle://` links across the Cycle app. Internal `cycle://` references should navigate inside the app without falling back to the browser, and external `cycle://` links should open Cycle and route to the referenced repository, ticket, room, board, or other supported destination.

## Problem
Cycle currently needs a consistent deep-link contract so repository and ticket references can be shared, rendered, clicked, and opened reliably. Links should work both inside Cycle chat/UI surfaces and from outside the app, such as Slack, GitHub, browser pages, documents, and terminal output.

## Goals
- Define the canonical `cycle://` URI formats that the app supports.
- Route internal `cycle://` clicks through the app navigation layer instead of treating them as normal external links.
- Register the desktop app as the OS handler for external `cycle://` links.
- When an external link opens Cycle, focus an existing app instance when possible and navigate to the resolved target.
- Handle unresolved, missing, unauthorized, deleted, or malformed links with a clear fallback state.

## Proposed Scope
- Add a shared parser/validator for supported `cycle://` URI shapes.
- Add internal click handling for markdown-rendered links and other app surfaces that may display Cycle references.
- Add main-process protocol registration for the desktop app, including cold-start and already-running app paths.
- Bridge external protocol open events into renderer navigation after app initialization.
- Add a single routing entry point that can resolve protocol targets and navigate to the relevant view.
- Add telemetry or debug logging for invalid links and failed resolutions if the app already has an established pattern.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking a `cycle://repository/<repositoryId>` link inside Cycle opens the matching repository context in-app.
- Clicking a `cycle://repository/<repositoryId>/tickets/<ticketId>` link inside Cycle opens the matching ticket in-app.
- External `cycle://` links open the Cycle desktop app on supported platforms.
- If Cycle is already running, external links focus the existing app instance and navigate without spawning a duplicate usable session.
- External links received during cold start are queued until the renderer is ready, then routed exactly once.
- Invalid or inaccessible links show a user-facing error or empty state instead of failing silently.
- The parser rejects malformed protocol URLs and does not allow arbitrary external navigation.
- Unit coverage exists for URI parsing and routing target extraction.
- Integration or manual QA notes cover macOS, Windows, and Linux protocol registration behavior where applicable.

## Notes / Open Questions
- Confirm all supported target types before implementation: repositories, tickets, rooms, boards, and project memory references.
- Decide whether protocol links should support workspace/account IDs to disambiguate references across multiple signed-in contexts.
- Decide whether web builds should also intercept `cycle://` links or only desktop builds should own protocol handling.

frontmatter.title: Support cycle:// protocol links internally and externally

value.title: Support cycle:// protocol links internally and externally
